name: Ted Lewis
abstract: Ted Lewis (June 6, 1890 – August 25, 1971) was an American entertainer, bandleader, singer, and musician. He was well known for his catchphrase "Is everybody happy?" He fronted a band and touring stage show that presented a combination of hot jazz, comedy, and nostalgia that was a hit with the American public before and after World War I…

concerts.label: Benefit: Haym Solomon Home for the Aged and Infirm

about_quotes.speaker: Sol Yaged
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Goodman's first job, Tim, was doing an imitation of Ted Lewis. That was his first job, Monk, his first paying job.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Netto
about_quotes.evidence: In fact I played with Ted Lewis down in the room there... And I was his official bass player.

about_quotes.speaker: Georg Brunis
about_quotes.evidence: I've stayed with him for 17 years, off and on... I was three times with Ted Lewis.

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: I went along with him to Frisco for a two-week contract at the Club Bimbo and I worked with him, doing 'Me and My Shadow.'

about_quotes.speaker: George "Pops" Foster and Mugsy Spanier
about_quotes.evidence: And I went with Ted Lewis. ... From Ray Miller, yeah, I went to Ted Lewis.
about_quotes.doc_title: George "Pops" Foster and Mugsy Spanier Interview, 1957-04-21

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Because before that, the racial mixing would be like Ted Lewis doing 'Me and My Shadow' -- he would prance around the stage signing 'Me and My Shadow' and the little negro boy would be following him and imitating every motion and be the shadow.
